childNodes.textContent:
Kitsoki mesa-sun favicon, inlined as a data URI: the SPA is built as a
         single go:embed'd file (vite-plugin-singlefile), so an external
         /favicon.svg reference would not be served. Source of truth:
         docs/branding/assets/mesa-sun-simple.svg (the small-size glyph).

Kitsoki "Mesa Sun" mark (docs/branding/logo.md, mesa-sun-simple.svg).
         Inlined as an SVG data URI so it survives vite-plugin-singlefile's
         single-file bundle and ships inside index.html — the Go web server
         (internal/runstatus/server) only routes "/" and "/rpc/*", so an
         external /favicon.svg would 404.

childNodes.textContent: deliver — epic front-door of the delivery loop

attributes.title: Meta mode — edit or ask about this story / kitsoki

node.textContent: A design becomes a work plan

Phase 2 published the speaker-notes-export design on the slidey repo. Now the deliver loop fans it into an executable plan — decomposing the epic into dependency-ordered, gate-bearing briefs, linting them, and loading a validated work plan. No LLM in the loop.

This is the deliver story: the epic front-door of the delivery loop. It takes the speaker-notes-export epic and turns it into a decomposition at .artifacts/deliver/decomposition.yaml — briefs the fleet can ship.
Click New session. The run opens already configured with the speaker-notes-export epic seeded — ready to decompose.
↳ Click the highlighted control to continue.
The deliver front-door opens with the speaker-notes-export epic already configured (its path seeded). One step — start — decomposes it into briefs, lints them, and loads the work plan.

node.textContent: The deliver front-door opens with the speaker-notes-export epic already configured (its path seeded). One step — start — decomposes it into briefs, lints them, and loads the work plan.

attributes.title: Story definition is up-to-date with the file on disk

node.textContent: Deliver an epic end-to-end: decompose it into briefs → lint → fan ship-it over every brief.

attributes.title: set the epic path and begin decomposition

node.textContent: set the epic path and begin decomposition

Start fans the epic into three dependency-ordered briefs — a SceneNotes model, a handout renderer, and a --notes CLI flag — each with its own go test gate. A structural lint passes, and the validated work plan loads into the fleet.

attributes.title: Call did not complete or its completion was not recorded
fan ship-it out over the briefs (merge-lock serializes)

node.textContent: fan ship-it out over the briefs (merge-lock serializes)

The run lands in fleet.load: three briefs loaded from .artifacts/deliver/decomposition.yaml, dependency-ordered and gate-bearing, ready for the fleet to ship slice by slice. The design is now an executable plan — no LLM spent.

node.textContent: No brief exceeds one agent's working set (single file + its test).

The design's three slices decompose into four right-sized, dependency-ordered briefs. Each is small enough for one agent to land end-to-end with its own test gate, and they fan out from a shared notes model.
unit test over a 3-scene fixture asserts order, fields, and the
golden snapshot is byte-stable across two runs. -
parse spec → run B1+B2 → write file (
writes the expected file; bad   spec exits non-zero with no partial write. -
invalid spec with a clear message. -
document the flag in the CLI reference and the authoring guide; add a
the flag is discoverable from the reference and the guide example   runs as written. -

flowchart LR
  B1[B1 model] --> B2[B2 renderer]
  B2 --> B3[B3 CLI]
  B3 --> B4[B4 docs]
| Brief | Size | Test gate | Can start | |---|---|---|---| | B1 | S | notes-model | now | | B2 | S | notes-render | after B1 | | B3 | M | notes-cli | after B2 | | B4 | S | guide example | after B3 |
Every brief carries its own test gate; the fleet runs them independently.
The manifest is byte-stable: same design in → same briefs out.
